violations.text: The floor in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization to monitor for new activity. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

Hummus, sliced tomatoes, and lettuce were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, cooler was not switched on this morning. Food was moved to walk-in freezer to cool at the time of inspection and cooler was switched on. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings along the wall in parts of the kitchen, ware washing room, mop sink closet, and dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. Establishment is to continue to work with pest control and have a pest control report available for the inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Sandwich pre unit was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Food at the bottom of prep unit was observed at 46 DF. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food was just prepped 30 minutes prior to inspection and was moved to properly functioning refrigeration at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in the bottom of the prep unit until the unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Walk-in cooler #2 was observed not in operation. Service was working on cooler at the time of inspection. Sandwich prep unit #1 was observed holding an ambient temperature of 46 DF at the time of inspection. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

General Tso's Chicken was observed held at 129 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Deli meats and cheese was observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, TCS foods were put in unit this morning. PIC relocated to another refrigerator at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Prep unit (sandwich line) was not in good repair, at time of inspection temperature was observed at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler fans have an accumulation of dust and dirt. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

Rice and beans, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Rice and beans observed held at 45-47 degrees in salad bar. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024

Smoothies, parfaits, salads, hummus packs, and sandwiches packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener base, and area beneath hot hold unit on service line have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

The fans, fan covers, fan soffit, and floors in the walk in coolers have an accumulation of dust, mold, and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was taken to dishroom. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2023

Hamburgers and chicken sandwiches observed held at 112-121 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Sandwiches were reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2023

Turkey, ham, cheese, and pepperoni were observed held at 46-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were placed in freezer to rapidly chill before being placed back in the prep units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2023

Smoothies, parfaits, salads, hummus packs, and sandwiches packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. English Food Label Approval Form New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2023

The fans, fan covers, fan cover soffit, and floors in the walk in coolers have an accumulation of dust, mold, and debris. Ceiling above the back food prep area have an accumulation of dust and soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2023
